zh.lifeSummary: 當Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières於1120年出生在法國安喬時，他的父親Gilbert Crispin Lord of Tillières21歲，母親Juliana Aigle13歲。他和Aliénor de   Vitre至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1190年在法國諾門迪享年70歲。

nameMeanings.name: Crispin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Pedro, Alicia, Fernando, Manuel, Rafael, Adolfo, Alfredo, Ana, Areli, Candita.  French Adrien, Andre, Eugenie, Francoise.

English; Spanish (Crispín): from the personal name Middle English Crispin, Spanish Crispín, from Latin Crispinus, derived from crispus ‘curly-haired’ (see Crisp ). This name was especially popular in France in the early Middle Ages, having been borne by a Christian saint who was martyred at Soissons in AD c. 285 along with a companion, Crispinianus (whose name is a further derivative of the same word). Compare Crespin and 3 below.

English (of Norman origin): from Old French and Middle English crespin, crispin ‘curly-haired’, also found as Crepin; see Crippin . It is difficult to tell this apart from the personal name in 1 above. According to Lanfranc, archbishop of Canterbury (1070–1089), the Norman Gilbert Crispin was the first man to receive this nickname, and two of his sons adopted it as their surname. His grandson Gilbert Crispin became abbot of Westminster in 1085. Members of the Norman family were said to have inherited a shock of hair that stood up stiffly like a bristly pine, as though the name derived from Latin crispus pinus, but this derivation is a medieval invention.

variants.name: Crisp
meanings: English: nickname for a man with curly hair, from Middle English crisp, crips, scrip, Old English crisp, cryps (from Latin crispus), reinforced in Middle English by an Old French word also from Latin crispus. In some cases this is possibly a nickname from the personal name Crispin .
Americanized form of German Krisp: nickname from Middle High German krisp, krispel ‘curly-haired man’.
Americanized form of German Krisp: from a short form of the medieval personal name Krispin (see Crispin ).

variants.name: Crippen
meanings: English: variant of   Crispin  .
Irish: variant of   Gribbin  .

variants.name: Crespin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Raul, Vidal, Adelina, Alfonso, Amador, Andres, Aniceto, Delfino, Emilio.
Spanish (Crespín): variant of Crispin .
French: from the Latin personal name Crispinus (see Crispin ).

variants.name: Crispen
meanings: English: variant of   Crispin  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain.
Americanized form of German Krispin, a cognate of Crispin .

variants.name: Crispino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Luigi, Carlo, Amato, Angelo, Antonio, Domenico, Fiore, Gennaro, Michelina, Rocco, Sal, Salvator.
Italian (southern): from the personal name Crispino, from Latin Crispinus, a derivative of the old Roman surname Crispus ‘curly(-headed)’ (see Crispin ).

variants.name: Crippin
meanings: English: variant of   Crispin  .
Irish: variant of   Gribbin  .

variants.name: Crespi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jaime, Juan, Mercedes, Racquel, Alberto, Alejandro, Consuelo, Eduardo, Jose, Jose Manuel, Migdalia, Pedro.  Jewish Aron, Nissim.
Catalan (Crespí): from a personal name, the Catalan equivalent of Crispin .
Italian (northern): variant of   Crespo  .

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières was born in 1120, in Anjou, France, his father, Gilbert Crispin Lord of Tillières, was 21 and his mother, Juliana Aigle, was 13. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Aliénor de   Vitre. He died in 1190, in Normandy, France, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières est né en 1120, à Anjou, France, son père, Gilbert Crispin Lord of Tillières, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Juliana Aigle, avait 13 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles avec Aliénor de   Vitre. Il est décédé en 1190, à Normandie, France, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières 1120 in Anjou, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gilbert Crispin Lord of Tillières 21 und seine Mutter Juliana Aigle 13. Er hatte mit Aliénor de   Vitre mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1190 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Normandie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières è nato il nel 1120 nel Anjou, Francia, il suo padre, Gilbert Crispin Lord of Tillières, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Juliana Aigle, aveva 13 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie con Aliénor de   Vitre. Morì nel 1190, nel Normandie, Francia, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillièresが1120年にフランスアンジョウで生まれた時，父Gilbert Crispin Lord of Tillièresは21歳，母Juliana Aigleは13歳であった。彼はAliénor de   Vitreとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1190年にフランスノーマンディで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières(이)가 1120년 프랑스 앤조우에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert Crispin Lord of Tillières(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Juliana Aigle(은)는 13세였다. 그는 Aliénor de   Vitre(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1190년 프랑스 노먼디에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières nasceu em 1120, em Anjou, França, seu pai, Gilbert Crispin Lord of Tillières, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Juliana Aigle, tinha 13 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas com Aliénor de   Vitre. Ele faleceu em 1190, em Normandie, França, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières родился в 1120 г. в местности анджоу, Франция, его отцу по имени Gilbert Crispin Lord of Tillières было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Juliana Aigle, 13. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери от Aliénor de   Vitre. Он умер в 1190 г. в местности норманди, Франция в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gilbert IV Crispin seigneur de Tillières nació en 1120, en Anjou, Francia, su papá, Gilbert Crispin Lord of Tillières, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Juliana Aigle, tenía 13 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas con Aliénor de   Vitre. Murió en 1190, en Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 70 años.
